steps.title: Identify what the blank must do
steps.content: The subject of the main clause is "people in the Americas." The blank must supply a finite verb that can serve as the main verb of that clause. A finite verb is the only form that can anchor an independent clause and carry tense.

steps.title: Eliminate non-finite forms and confirm the answer
steps.content:
All four choices are verb forms, but three are non-finite and cannot anchor a main clause:
  • Choice A — to use: a to-infinitive — non-finite; leaves the clause without a main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — have used: present perfect — a finite form; pairs naturally with "for thousands of years" to express an action spanning from the past to the present. ✓
  • Choice C — having used: a participial form — non-finite; cannot anchor a main clause. ✗
  • Choice D — using: a participial form — non-finite; cannot anchor a main clause. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
